vulnerabilities.description: strdup(username) can return NULL on heap allocation failure; the return value is stored in s->username without a NULL check, leaving a NULL pointer that will be dereferenced on later access.

strncpy copies exactly strlen(token) bytes with no room for a NUL terminator; when strlen(token) == 32 the token field is left unterminated, corrupting adjacent memory on subsequent string operations.

session_free frees the Session and its username but does not require the caller to NULL the pointer; any caller that retains the pointer after this call holds a dangling pointer enabling use-after-free.
s is dereferenced without a NULL check; if the caller passed a freed (non-NULL but dangling) pointer, this constitutes a use-after-free, and a NULL pointer would cause an immediate crash.
session_logout(s) frees s via session_free but does not NULL the caller's pointer; the immediate call to session_get_user(s) on the same line reads from the freed Session object, a use-after-free.
When command is not "logout", admin_action returns without freeing the Session pointed to by s, leaking the entire Session allocation including the heap-allocated username.

cross_function.description: admin_action calls session_logout which calls session_free(s), freeing the Session; admin_action then immediately passes the now-freed s pointer to session_get_user, reading from deallocated memory.
session_create allocates the Session and its username; when admin_action is called with any command other than "logout", neither admin_action nor any callee frees the Session, permanently leaking it.
